meta.ablation_anchor: AT HEAD daa79cd25ca (the active editable torch at /tmp/pytorch-work; == A0 ab_target_B)
meta.method: HEAD-default (control) vs HEAD-with-one-flag-toggled (marginal contribution). Locked path only: scripts/bench_parallel.py (repro path for corpus, --full-graphs for genai), INDUCTOR_GPU_BENCH_LOCK + do_bench min-of-100. Fresh/separate TORCHINDUCTOR_CACHE_DIR per arm.

noise_floor.method: two independent control runs (HEAD default, fresh caches), same 60 shape-points
noise_floor.note: geomean noise ~0.2%; per-shape noise up to ~9% on sub-10us kernels. A geomean within ~1% of 1.0, or a per-shape move under ~9% on a tiny kernel, is AT FLOOR. n_kernels change is the structural tell of a real codegen difference.

env_var_mechanism_verification.how: bench_parallel._locked_worker builds env=os.environ.copy() and passes it to the worker subprocess (subprocess.Popen(..., env=env)); the worker imports torch._inductor.config which binds TORCHINDUCTOR_* at import. Verified U09: TORCHINDUCTOR_LINEAR_REDUCTION_ELIMINATION=0 flips config.linear_reduction_elimination True->False inside the worker, AND the per-pass counter + generated-Triton structure change accordingly.
env_var_mechanism_verification.caveat_found: The generated-source HASH alone is NOT a reliable affected-repro signal: inductor embeds a config-hash COMMENT in the output, so the hash differs even when the pass did not fire. The authoritative signal is counters['inductor'][<pass>] (>0 = pass fired = repro affected) and/or n_kernels change. Corrected the proxy to use counters.
env_var_mechanism_verification.u25_not_env_bound: config.inline_recomputable_producers is a plain `= True` with NO Config(env_name_...) binding; generic TORCHINDUCTOR_INLINE_RECOMPUTABLE_PRODUCERS does NOTHING (verified). Ablated instead via a usercustomize.py on the worker's PYTHONPATH that sets the flag False at interpreter startup when ABLATE_U25_OFF=1 — NOT a new timer, just a config patch in the worker before any compile. Verified it flips the flag inside a worker-equivalent process AND changes n_kernels on real repros.

affected_set_discovery.method: corpus-wide counter scan: compile each of 1727 canonical repros once under default config (U01 with scatter_reduce_fusion forced ON, since its HEAD default is OFF) and record every pass counter. counter>0 => affected.

U01_scatter_reduce_fusion.note: ZERO fires across all 1727 repros EVEN WITH scatter_reduce_fusion=True. The masked/bilinear scatter-reduce-gather pattern it targets does not exist in this corpus. Its only evidence is the self-reported microbench in A0.

U08_as_strided_scatter_elision.families: CNN BN-reduction (nfnet, mobilenet, convnext, inception, ghostnet, visformer, repvgg, mobilevit, efficientnet)

U25_inline_recomputable_producers.note: fires on 43% of the corpus (pervasive scheduler decision), but is performance-neutral on the vast majority: only ~2/66 sampled shapes change n_kernels.

U01.genai: counter probe: zero fires on all 8 micros
U01.verdict: DROP-on-corpus (zero corpus footprint). Self-reported microbench only. Land only if its target workload (masked/bilinear scatter-reduce gather) is in scope elsewhere; otherwise no corpus/genai evidence supports it.

U07.interpretation: AT NOISE FLOOR (geomean 1.0067 vs noise 1.0017). One real structural win: pointwise_656833e29d89 (pyhpc_isoneutral) nk 30->61 when off, 1.33x; pyhpc_turbulent (pointwise_43588d3e9780) nk 91->87, 1.07x. But the 13 Longformer shapes are flat (1.00x, nk unchanged) and 5 shapes show >3% moves in BOTH directions (noise). Net geomean is indistinguishable from zero.
U07.per_model_e2e: negligible: the 2 structural wins are isolated pyhpc shapes; the Longformer mass is unaffected.
U07.verdict: BUNDLE (below 0.82pp floor on corpus). Keep for the pyhpc structural cases but do not land as a standalone perf PR; ship inside a scatter-elision family PR.

U08.interpretation: AT NOISE FLOOR (1.0043 vs 1.0017). 5 small CNN-BN shapes help 1.3-9% (nk drops, e.g. 1->3 when off), but ONE shape is NET-NEGATIVE: tf_efficientnet_b0 0.748 (off is 25% FASTER) — the elision induced a worse kernel there. Mixed; geomean ~ noise.
U08.per_model_e2e: negligible/mixed: small absolute kernels (14-220us); the efficientnet regression cancels the small wins.
U08.verdict: BUNDLE / borderline. Below floor and carries a per-shape regression (tf_efficientnet 0.748). Land only inside a scatter-elision family PR after gating out the efficientnet-shaped regression.

U09.interpretation: NET-NEGATIVE on corpus (0.9929 < 1.0 => turning the flag OFF is ~0.7% FASTER on average), though within noise. 4 BN-backward shapes hurt by the flag, 2 helped. n_kernels NEVER changes (identical codegen structure) — the flag only perturbs block-size/algebra and on this corpus it perturbs slightly wrong. All 6 affected repros are functorch_dp_cifar10.
U09.per_model_e2e: near-zero / slightly negative for functorch_dp_cifar10 BN-backward.
U09.genai: zero movement isolated (within +-1% on all 11 micro-graphs)
U09.verdict: DROP-net-negative (on corpus). Like BN-affine-fold (-0.29), the linear-reduction-elimination algebra is a wash-to-slight-loss on the measured corpus. Do not land standalone; needs a cost-model gate.

U10.interpretation: REAL, LARGE win, FAR above noise. 15/18 shapes slower when off (up to 1.80x). Structural: kernel count rises when off (1->3, 2->3, 6->5) — the store-sinking fuses the channel-shuffle layout transform into its producer. All 14 affected repros are shufflenet_v2_x1_0.
U10.per_model_e2e: concentrated but real: shufflenet_v2 layout-transform/var_mean kernels are 9-53us; a 1.2x kernel-geomean on the channel-shuffle kernels. Per-MODEL e2e impact bounded to ShuffleNetV2's layout-transform fraction (kernel-geomean OVERSTATES per-model, but the structural nk drop confirms a genuine fusion win).
U10.verdict: LAND-worthy (clearly above floor, structural, unambiguous). Only caveat: footprint is ShuffleNetV2-specific in this corpus; verify per-model-e2e on a ShuffleNet roll-up before claiming a corpus-wide pp.

U25.flag: config.inline_recomputable_producers (NO env binding; ablated via usercustomize config-patch). NOTE: inline_cheap_producers_into_stencils is FALSE at HEAD (zero contribution), so U25's only active half is inline_recomputable_producers.
U25.interpretation: NET-NEGATIVE and a MIXED BAG with REAL, REPRODUCIBLE large regressions. Geomean 0.9826 (off is ~1.7% faster). On 48-repro stratified sample (66 shape-points): 4 help >3%, 10 hurt >3%, 52 at floor. The extreme movers REPRODUCE on re-bench: pointwise_07c6ea330ab4 (Longformer) 1.36x HELP (nk 1->2); but sum_68fcffe5c7fb (XGLM) 0.66x, any_amax_amax_af50781fc699 (XLNet) 0.62x, sum_02fe2c82b1fd (GPTNeo) 0.77x are all the flag making transformer reductions 25-60% SLOWER. The XLNet/XGLM regressions have nk 1->1 (same kernel count, worse heuristics from the inlining).
U25.per_model_e2e: NEGATIVE on several transformers (XLNet, XGLM, GPTNeo). Matches A1 cross-check: pytorch_unet is -0.50% AT the mega-commit (the 'R1 realization family' A0 flagged). The fix-chain (A0's 8 follow-up commits) did NOT fully neutralize the transformer regressions measured here.
U25.verdict: DROP-net-negative as-is / needs-selective-revert-of-fix-chain. The producer-inlining is a per-pattern win (Longformer 1.36x) buried under per-pattern losses (transformer reductions 0.6-0.8x). Do NOT land the bare flag; it needs the cost-model gate that distinguishes the Longformer stencil case from the transformer-reduction case. This is the unit most in need of decomposition.

U30.flag: NONE (simd.py _emit_triton_finalize_sum +176, unconditional; _sibling_reduction_fusion is False at HEAD)
U30.verdict: REQUIRES SELECTIVE-REVERT (not env-ablatable). The MOR finalize-kernel codegen is unconditional. Mega-commit claims 35% Swin. Cannot be flag-ablated; benching requires reverting the simd.py +176 hunk on a worktree (out of scope for env-ablation). NOTE: counter probe shows it does NOT fire on the 8 genai micros, so genai is NOT the right surface for U30 either; the right surface is Swin (a model roll-up), which needs a selective-revert arm.

U31.flag: NONE (cross_entropy_loss_online prim + new reduction type; always active on pattern match)
U31.verdict: REQUIRES SELECTIVE-REVERT. The genai CrossEntropyForward/Backward micros are the right surface, but the feature has no flag — the CE pattern either lowers to the online fused reduction or not. To attribute, revert the prim+ops_handler+lowering+triton CE hunks together on a worktree and re-bench CrossEntropy* via the locked --full-graphs path. Mega-commit claims 0.8%.

U33.flag: PARTIAL: TORCHINDUCTOR_PERSISTENT_REDUCTION_THRESHOLD_INNER is an env-tunable SCALAR (not a feature on/off); the Blackwell-BN threshold raise + low-warp configs + cooperative-widen are NO-FLAG code changes.
U33.verdict: REQUIRES SELECTIVE-REVERT (mostly). The d2cd/62f27 INNER-threshold 1024->8192 was added+reverted (net zero, A2 confirms DROP-MOOT). The Blackwell-BN + low-warp config additions are autotune-config code changes that need a selective revert arm, not an env toggle.

reconciliation.marginal_sum_of_6_ablatable_on_corpus: Of the 6 env/config-ablatable units, only U10 clears the noise floor with a real positive (1.20x kernel-geomean, ShuffleNetV2-localized). U01=zero footprint; U07,U08 at floor (mixed); U09 net-slightly-negative; U25 NET-NEGATIVE with reproducible transformer regressions. So the 6 ablatable units do NOT sum to a large positive on the corpus — the mega-commit's +1.95pp kernel-geomean must come substantially from the 3 NO-FLAG units (U30 MOR/Swin 35%-claim, U31 CE, U33 Blackwell-BN/low-warp) which this corpus+genai-flag surface cannot capture, PLUS U10's localized win.
net_negative_units: U25 (inline_recomputable_producers, 0.9826 + reproducible XLNet 0.62x / XGLM 0.66x / GPTNeo 0.77x regressions)
reconciliation.does_it_reconcile: PARTIALLY, and it REFRAMES the mega-commit. The walk's +1.95pp kernel-geomean / +0.71pp e2e is NOT evenly spread across the 9 units. On the measured corpus+genai-flag surface: U10 is the only clean positive; U25 is net-negative (and is the source of the A1 pytorch_unet -0.50% regression AT the mega-commit). The bulk of the claimed +1.95pp is therefore attributable to the NO-FLAG units (U30 Swin-MOR, U31 CE, U33 BN/low-warp) whose surfaces (Swin model roll-up, the CE-pattern) are NOT exercised by env-ablation. CONCLUSION for the A2 plan: do NOT land the mega-commit's flags as an undifferentiated bundle — U10 lands clean, U07/U08 bundle below floor, U01 is corpus-dead, and U09+U25 are net-negative and must be gated/decomposed (U25 most urgently).

provenance.pytorch_work_head: daa79cd25ca (UNTOUCHED; third_party/* submodule-pointer dirt is pre-existing, not written by this ablation)
provenance.worktrees_created: none (existing worktrees in `git worktree list` predate this work)
